🎬↔📋 Protocol–Video Alignment — Claude vs VLM (our own demo)

Self-collected demo: IMG_2046.MOV (PCR mutagenesis setup, 156s), 24 frames, reviewed by Claude · claude-opus-4-8 and Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct side by side with a Claude↔VLM agreement strip. No ground-truth timestamps were collected, so this is a qualitative comparison (not scored vs truth).

1. Thaw Q5 Hot Start Master Mix, forward primer, reverse primer, plasmid template, and nuclease-free water.
2. Label 2 PCR tubes (Negative Control, Mutagenesis)
3. Prepare the PCR master mix for 2 reactions (12 µL Q5 Hot Start Master Mix, 2 µL Forward Primer (10 µM), 2 µL Reverse Primer (10 µM), 2 µL nuclease-free water)
4. Mix the master mix gently until homogeneous.
5. Add 6 µL master mix to each PCR tube.
6. Add to Mutagenesis 2 µL plasmid template
7. Add to Negative Control 2 µL nuclease-free water
8. Gently pipette-mix each reaction.
9. Briefly spin tubes to collect liquid at the bottom.
10. Place tubes into the thermocycler.

What it observed
A gloved researcher in a lab coat works at a bench stocked with pipettes, yellow tube racks holding small labeled tubes, a reagent bottle, tip boxes, and an Erlenmeyer flask. Early on (frame 0) he poses with pipettes; in frame 1 he uses a marker, apparently writing/labeling near the yellow rack. Frames 2–12 show extended single-channel pipetting between tubes in the yellow racks — drawing and dispensing small volumes repeatedly, consistent with preparing a master mix and distributing it plus adding reagents. Frame 13 shows tubes being placed into a small benchtop mini-centrifuge (myFUGE). Frame 14 shows the researcher operating a benchtop thermocycler touchscreen and loading it. Frame 15 is a closing shot of him smiling. No explicit thawing step or distinct reagent identification (template vs water) is clearly visible.
